input.layout_description: Peach's Castle is the first location in the map. The player can buy and watch hint movies here.

The first level of New Super Mario Bros. Wii . It introduces the Propeller Suit , Goombas and Koopa Troopas across rolling hills in grassy mushroom plains. There are large mushroom hills in the background.
The first underground level of New Super Mario Bros. Wii . It introduces the Ice Flower and Piranha Plants , and is full of moving crystal blocks and Warp Pipes .
This grassy level introduces Yoshi , Venus Fire Traps and the only appearance of Hammer Bros . There are many bushes with fruits Yoshi can eat off of them, and mushroom hills in the distance.

meta.level_code: A Warp Cannon inWorld 1ofNew Super Mario Bros. Wii

input.layout_description: The Warp Cannon of World 1, which leads directly to World 5 the jungle world. It is unlocked by finding the hidden goal in World 1-3.
The first tower level of New Super Mario Bros. Wii . It has many moving stone blocks and Pendulum Platforms and introduces Dry Bones . The boss is Larry Koopa .

The first underwater level of New Super Mario Bros. Wii . It introduces Cheep Cheeps , Deep-Cheeps , Eep Cheeps , and Clampys; and many jet bubble pipes making swimming harder. It takes place in a lake in the plains.

Takes place high in the clouds, with giant mushrooms and spinning blocks amongst the mushroom hills. It contains regular and screw-controlled mushrooms, as well as spinning blocks. This level introduces Koopa Paratroopas .
A grassland level containing many rolling hills. Koopa Troopas and Koopa Paratroopas are the principal enemies. There are secrets hidden climbing vines and down pipes.
The first castle level of New Super Mario Bros. Wii . It contains lava pools, Thwomps and giant spinning gears. Its boss is Larry Koopa again now with moving crushing platforms.
This level takes place in the desert and is the first level with sand geysers . There are giant pyramids in the background, and sandstone blocks across the sand.

This level is filled with barrels and Spikes . There are many sandy slopes for the barrels and spikes to roll. There are areas with platforms infested with Koopas and Spikes. Beneath the sandstone are caves dripping with water.
This level is a pitch dark cave, which is dimly lit by lanterns. Fire Bros ., Fire Snakes , Venus Fire Traps and Spike Tops are common enemies in this level.
This tower features metallic fences that the player can cling on. The player will need to avoid spells from Roy coming from above.

In the sand desert, every few seconds, Wind blows in the level, forcing the player to the right. You can hide from the wind in hidden caves with pits; or cling on to rope ladders to not get blown back.

This level has a Lakitu flying overhead. It has many Pokeys as well as some Lakitus , and there's also a Yoshi in this level. You jump across pits of quicksand, using Yoshi to eat the Pokeys and Spinies thrown by the Lakitu.
This is a mushroom level in the sky that mostly has moving coins and blocks. It takes place over the desert.
The Warp Cannon level of World 2. It shoots the player straight into World 5 the jungle world.
This castle is filled with lava pits, and three way corridors, of which only one is the correct path. You must follow the correct path to find the castle's secrets. The boss is Roy Koopa .

The level starts in a snowy ground with Cooligans sliding in Mario's direction. Huge snow covered pines are in the background, with icy ground and snow covered hills loom in the distance. There are huge ice slides cliffs to complete the level.
This level marks the first appearance of Ice Bros . and Bullet Bills in the game. You must dodge the bullet bills between the pink trees scattered across the level.
This level is an icy cave level with three parts. There are floating platforms in icy water, falling Icicles , and giant falling icicles that Mario can walk on.
This is the first Ghost House in the game. Big Boos, smaller Boos, and possessed furniture are the main obstacles in this level.
The Warp Cannon level of World 3. It shoots the player straight into World 6 the mountain cliffs.

This level features brightly colored rotating blocks that must be used to climb to higher locations. There are also huge platforms that ascend the tower, with many Dry Bones and Amps to dodge. This is also the only level to feature Amps .

This is an icy field with many icy blocks and platforms. There are hidden blocks in this level which will turn into solid red blocks when the ! Switch is activated further in the game. There is a snowy forest in the background.

This is a vertical level, and will only autoscroll when all players step on the rotating pink block. You ascend higher and higher into dark snowy clouds, climbing mushrooms and floating warp pipes, dodging ice and fire alike.

Lemmy Koopa 's castle is riddled with giant falling icicles and frozen floors making timed jumps difficult. The player must move on Ice Snake Blocks throughout the level. There are many frozen Bob-ombs which explode the paths as well. Lemmy Koopa launched giant bouncy balls at the player to knock them off.

A vibrant tropical level introducing Big Urchins that is mostly in water. Swim and jump across the beaches and wooden platforms in the sun. There is tropical greenery growing atop of pale hills in the background.
Most of this course is filled with Donut Blocks and Cheep Cheeps that will jump up to attack the player. You run along stony isles in the water, with vibrant greenery in the distance.
This is the only level that features Huckit Crabs . There are also many Urchins and Big Urchins. You jump between many sandy or rocky isles, swimming in the pleasant tide.
This tower features many conveyor belts . Iron Block are also a common sight here, being used as both platforms and obstacles. The conveyor belts carry iron blocks that try to crush the player.
The cannon level of World 4. It takes the player straight to World 6 .
You go underwater in the tropics, with many Bloopers patroling the waters. Mega Cheep-Chomps swim around the geyser pipes, as you swim through tropical and colorful waters plus giant seaweed.
This is the Ghost House of World 4. Throughout are many Scaredy Rats, which march in long lines and hide behind doors. Its hidden goal is a shortcut to World 4-Castle .
A beach level with Yoshi and Lakitus . The beaches are covered in palm trees, and there are many fruits and spinies for Yoshi to eat.

This is the castle level of World 4 that has the only appearance of fences and Climbing Koopas . There are also many giant skewers that attack between the fences. And there is an underwater section at the bottom of the castle filled with Wet Bones.
This is the first airship level of the game. The airship flies over the tropical sea, and is filled with many moving torches and flamethrowers. MechaKoopas patrol the ship. Bowser Jr. is fought here.

It introduces the Stalking Piranha Plant and the River Piranha Plant. There are large Koopa Shells throughout the level. While most of this level takes place on land, a portion of it is over a body of water. There are many hanging vines that Mario and co. can use to travel over gaps and Stalking Piranha Plants.
Starting above-ground, there are many wigglers wandering across various platforms. Eventually you go underground and find a massive pool of poison, with huge wigglers walking through the caves.
Over a lake of poison water, the player avoids Bramballs that roam the islands. You jump between logs over the poison. This is the only stage where Bramballs appear.
This tower contains several moving platforms jutting out of spiked walls moving right and left.The spiked walls shift left and right as you ascend the tower.

In this level, Mario must travel on a plank to the pole to avoid the poison. The plank only moves if there are less than 5 entities on it. Goombas, Bob-ombs and Koopa Troopas constantly fall from above to halt your progress.

In this autoscrolling sky level, the player first jumps on one of the backs of a giant green Jumbo Ray. They have platforms to help them just in case they lose a Jumbo Ray. There are also Propeller Blocks that help them. There are a lot of Bullet Bills in the level that are fired at the player from off-screen.

Common Ghost House enemies can be found, including Boos. More of a maze than a typical ghost house, this house has a multitude of doors in each room; with some paths instead leading to dead ends. The level is subsumed in darkness which you use light blocks to illuminate.
You jump across giant spinning fences over lava, where you climb against the grating to dodge the lava bubbles. Iggy Koopa fights you with his giant Chain Chomp.
input.boss: Iggy Koopa and Giant Chain Chomp

input.layout_description: This level takes place on a series of cliffs populated by Stone Spikes and Bullet Bills.
A level taking place in a Piranha Plant -infested cavern, with many Rolling Rock Hills throughout.
A sewer-themed level mainly featuring Super Piranha Plants , Stalking Piranha Plants , and Buzzy Beetles . You go through many pipes and swim through water systems.

This level mainly takes place on high semisolid cliffs, which Mario and Yoshi can maneuver. This level has the first and only appearance of Monty Moles . They constantly jump out of holes to attack! You also jump across balance lifts.
The primary feature of World 6's Fortress is two giant Skewers on the floor and ceiling, which both collide in the middle of the Fort.

This tropical-themed level primarily features Porcupuffers jumping out of the water. Some large ? Blocks also make an appearance later on. This hidden mountain oasis has rocky isles with rampaging Porcupuffers.
This dimly-lit cavern level has Mario ride a raft, with floodlights that can be rotated with the Wii Remote. Common enemies here include Mega Cheep-Cheeps and Swoops .
The cannon level of World 6. The cannon will shoot straight to World 8, the lava lair.
World 6's Castle primarily features spiked balls and Giant Spiked Balls on pendulum platforms. Spikes are everywhere!

World 6's airship features Mechakoopas and has the first and only appearance of Rocky Wrenches . Bowser Jr.'s battle primarily features the introduction (and only appearance) of the Remote-Controlled Clown Car . There are many spinning platforms that protude fromn the ship.
In the clouds, the level features different colored spinning blocks which move at different speeds. Also, colorful moving pipes are scattered around, along with a lot of Piranha Plants.
This level features floating sky islands where floating water balls hover over. There are many chain chomps guarding the islands.
This level takes place in the clouds across many colorful mushrooms. Many fuzzies big and small ride on rails between the mushroom platforms.

You ascend a tower with a tilt lift, going both inside and outside of the cloudy tower. Barrages of bullet bills and banzai bills attack you, and bob-ombs are launched from cannons. Ludwig Von Koopa operates this industrial gauntlet.

World 7-Ghost House is the last Ghost House in New Super Mario Bros. Wii. It is the only one to feature Broozers (which are mostly placed near barrels). Crowbers also appear outside the Ghost House near the end of the level. There is also a hidden goal in this course. All coins and blocks in this level slowly swing from left to right.
This level takes place high in the sky across many floating warp pipes. Bullet bills are launched at you constantly. There are also many moving floating platforms.
This floating sky cave is filled with Foos which constantly blow clouds and wind against the player. Jumping across floating platforms between the sky islands has you dodge the foos and paragoombas.

This sky level is a shortcut to the Castle, where you climb across the shells of flying Para-Beetles, which go up or down when you land on them. It takes place between small sky islands ascending up in the sky.

This castle takes place outside, against a sky backdrop, with huge industrial metal platforms, and giant skewers between them. Sledge Bros. guard the various spires of the castle. Ludvig Von Koopa is fought at the end, on rapidly climbing the highest peak of the tower.
This level features volcanic ash and volcanic debris, taking place over a large field of lava, with volcanoes in the distance raining down debris .

This level contains more falling debris and spinning wheels on the ground. This level also contains a secret exit . There are many spike balls rolling along the rocky spinning wheels. It takes place in a lava filled canyon of dark rock.
This level features giant lava waves that will instantly defeat the player. You avoid them in the lava cave by jumping across metal tilting platforms suspended by chains.

This tower is the first level to feature Fire Bars . Unlike the previous towers in this game, the background is tinted red, with ember particles throughout. There are also many stone blocks that shift as platforms. Magikoopa is the boss and attacks with many spells and different varieties of enemy.
This level takes place in an dimly-lit underwater cave, and features Jellybeams , Bulbers , and Stone Spikes . Jellybeams and Bulbers light up the dark waters.

This level is an auto-scrolling level over lava, featuring Tilt Lifts that can be moved by rotating the Wii Remote . Bob-ombs rain down from the sky, and Crowbers assault the player. Lava geysers spew and the player constantly has to balance the platform and avoiding enemies.
This level takes place in a volcano with rising lava. You must ascend the volcano to escape the rising magma, using trampolines, propellor blocks and suits, and swinging stone platforms.

This level is a secret level, and can only be unlocked by taking the secret path on World 8-2. It features a difficult ride over a sea of lava, atop of the Spine Coaster. With the guttural scream, the Spine Coaster travels at pace over the lava, between the cliffs and caves and lava geysers.

This is the last airship level of the game. it has more cannons, loosing cannon balls everywhere. There are many hanging platforms that you must unscrew to make your way beneath the ship. The final fight with Bowser Jr inside of the giant Koopa Clown Car.

This is the final castle level of the game and is home to Bowser . It is an enormous castle, full of falling bone platforms, fire bars and lava bubbles. There are enormous metal statues of Bowser. When you defeat Bowser on the bridge, Kamek makes him supersized and a colossal chase through the lava tunnels take place.
The first level of World 9 is sky-themed and has the mushroom mountain sky background from World 1 . There are spinning rectangle platforms throughout the level.
This stage takes place in the beach setting from World 4 . The player must get to the goal while avoiding a Porcupuffer and many Koopa Troopas, by swinging across vines and jumping across the water .
This course takes place on sky islands inbetween giant mountains . It features a heavy arsenal Missile Bills and Bull's-Eye Banzais . There are many moving platforms, over giant pits in the sky.

This course takes place over the Desert Theme of World 2 . It mainly revolves around both Bob-ombs and Venus Fire Traps . There are many pipes over the sandy skies of the desert, with brick blocks and Bob-ombs to break.
This is an icy course based on World 3 , where the player must get to the top of the Icy Mountain while avoiding Cooligans , Fuzzies , and Big Fuzzies .
This volcanic course is based on the lava lair in World 8 and contains numerous platforms which emerge from and descend into lava . The fiery gauntlet takes place over a sea of lava.

This course, based on the World 5 Jungle , takes place in a snowy forest, with floors made of ice blocks containing Munchers and Warp Pipes containing Venus Fire Traps . It is the melding of fire and ice.
The last course of the game is an auto-scrolling sky level, akin to the cloudy World 7 , which features King Bills as well as Goombas and Fuzzies. There are many colorful bricks and bouncy clouds.
